title: muehlenbeckia urubambensis
intro: Muehlenbeckia urubambensis, also known as Urubamba vine, is a type of flowering plant in the family Polygonaceae. The Urubamba vine has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other secondary metabolites. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating gastrointestinal issues. In traditional Andean medicine, the Urubamba vine is prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is also used as a topical application in the form of a salve or ointment.
